title.en: Must be nice! I'm so jealous! 진짜 좋겠다! 부러워!

description.en: When something good happens to a friend! Practice expressing honest and playful envy with 진짜 좋겠다 and 부러워.
description.ko: 친구에게 좋은 일이 생겼을 때! 진짜 좋겠다와 부러워를 사용해 솔직하면서도 장난스러운 부러움을 표현해 봅니다.

tags: Reaction. Envy. Jealousy. Friendship. Positive. Daily Life

content.speaker: John
content.text: What's up world! Welcome back. You are listening to... "Real Reaction!" I'm your host. John.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존! 저 방금 이벤트 당첨 문자를 받았어요.
content.translation: John! I just got an event win text message.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 이번에 새로 나온 최신형 태블릿 PC를 공짜로 받게 됐대요!
content.translation: They say I'm getting a brand new tablet PC for free!

content.speaker: John
content.text: What? For free? Oh man. must be nice!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 와 미나 진짜 좋겠다! 전 그런 운은 하나도 없는데 너무 부러워요.
content.translation: Wow. you must be so lucky. Mina! I have no luck like that. so I'm so jealous.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 저도 이런 적은 처음이라 너무 얼떨떨해요.
content.translation: This is the first time for me too. so I'm very dazed.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존이 부러워 하니까 더 실감이 나네요!
content.translation: It feels more real since you're jealous. John!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 진짜 좋겠다. This is used to express envy about someone else's good situation. right.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 네. 상대방에게 일어난 긍정적인 일을 보고 "나도 저렇게 됐으면 좋겠다" 라는 마음을 담은 리액션이에요.
content.translation: Yes. It's a reaction containing the feeling of "I wish I were like that too" after seeing something positive happen to someone.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 한국 사람들이 정말 입에 달고 사는 아주 대중적인 표현이죠.
content.translation: It's a very popular expression that Koreans say all the time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 진짜 좋겠다.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Then what about 부러워.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 이건 직접적으로 나의 부러운 감정을 말할 때 써요.
content.translation: This is used when you directly state your feeling of envy.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "부러우면 지는 거다" 라는 농담이 있을 정도로 가끔은 귀엽게 질투하는 느낌을 줄 수도 있어요.
content.translation: There's even a joke saying "If you're jealous. you lose." Sometimes it can give a feeling of cute jealousy.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 부러워.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay. let's try a situation. Imagine I just told you that my company gave me a whole month of paid vacation.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존! 한 달 동안 유급 휴가를 받는다고요?
content.translation: John! You're getting a month of paid vacation?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 회사에서 정말 파격적인 대우를 해주네요. 진짜 좋겠다!
content.translation: The company is giving you such an exceptional treatment. Wow. must be nice!

content.speaker: John
content.text: I'm planning to travel to Jeju Island and just relax on the beach for 30 days.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 제주도에서 한 달 살기라니..., 진심으로 부러워요.
content.translation: Living in Jeju for a month... I'm sincerely jealous.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 제 부러움이 느껴지시나요? 전 내일도 출근해야 하는데 말이에요!
content.translation: Can you feel my envy? Since I have to go to work tomorrow too!

content.speaker: John
content.text: I'll send you lots of pictures from the beach. Mina!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Honest reactions like "I'm jealous" can actually make friends feel more special.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 시샘하기보다 솔직하게 부럽다고 말해주면 서로 기분이 더 좋아질 수 있어요.
content.translation: Right. Rather than being spiteful. saying honestly that you're jealous can make each other feel better.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 오늘 배운 표현들로 친구의 행복을 마음껏 축하하고 부러워해 보세요!
content.translation: Congratulate your friend's happiness and be jealous to your heart's content with the expressions we learned today!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review our "jealous" reactions one last time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 진짜 좋겠다! Must be nice!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 부러워! I'm so jealous!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 부러우면 지는 건데! If you're jealous. you lose!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 전생에 나라를 구했나 봐! You must have saved the country in your past life!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 정말 대박이다! That's so awesome!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분도 오늘 주변 사람들에게 "좋겠다!"라는 말을 들을 만큼 기분 좋은 일이 가득하시길 바랄게요.
content.translation: I hope you all have a day full of pleasant things enough to hear "Must be nice!" from people around you today.

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. See you in the next episode.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.meaning: To be envious. To be jealous
vocabulary.example: 친구의 새 차가 정말 부러워요. (I'm really envious of my friend's new car.)
이벤트 당첨 문자를 받고 깜짝 놀랐어요. (I was surprised to receive an event winning text message.)
이번 여름 휴가는 해외로 가고 싶어요. (I want to go abroad for this summer vacation.)
사소한 일로 친구를 질투하지 마세요. (Don't be jealous of your friend for trivial things.)
행운을 불러오는 비결이 따로 있나요? (Is there a specific secret to bringing good luck?)
당신에게 행운이 가득하길 빌게요. (I wish you all the best of luck.)
